Hover the mouse cursor over the speaker image in the lower right corner of the screen and right-click, then select the "Open sound settings" menu item, as shown in Figure 1.
Figure 1. Open sound settings.

A screen with the current sound settings will appear. Remember what is set for input and output audio devices. You will need this information after you finish working. In Figure 2, the red arrows show what you need to remember. In your case, the names will be different.
Figure 2. Initial sound settings
Set the sound output device to
Set the sound input device to
In Figure 3, the red arrows show what and where you need to set.
Figure 3. Working sound settings
System settings are complete, you can run the program

You speak the language you know, you are heard in the language your interlocutor understands. A log of your conversations is kept, which you can manage. All operations are performed locally on your computer running Windows 10 or Windows 11. A graphics processor is desirable, but not required, for the program to work. The quality of the translation is improved with a graphics processor.

If you understand one of the languages ​​from this list, you will be able to communicate with a native speaker of any other language from this list. Communication can be in Telegram, WhatsApp, Google meet, VKMessenger, YandexMessenger or any other messenger. You can listen to news, training courses and any other podcasts in any of the mentioned languages ​​and understand them. Now it is possible to pass interviews in foreign companies and much more. You can also play any audio / video file in a foreign language and hear it in your language. One of the side applications is conversations with the interlocutor in the same language, but your interlocutor will hear the voice generated by the neural network and not your own voice. Sometimes this is necessary. There are many possible applications.
Operating system Windows 10 or Windows 11.
Available hard drive space 8 GB.
RAM from 10 GB.
GPU (graphics processing unit) with 6 GB or more, desirable but not necessary.
Headphones with a microphon.
The entire interface of working with the program is carried out from the browser.
When you click the "Configuration" button, a form with the main operating parameters is displayed in the top menu. See Figure 4.
Figure 4. Configuration.
The Configuration section contains the main operating parameters:
Language - setting the language you speak.
Sound - the name of the driver for your sound-emitting device.
Microphone - the name of the driver for your microphone.
Web browser - the name of the browser in which you will be working.

When you change the language and save the configuration settings, all text, expressions, button names, and other text will be displayed in the new language. New chats will be translated into the selected language, and you will hear speech in this new language. Important note about the Web browser parameter. By default, the Web browser parameter is set to Microsoft Edge, since it is always installed in Windows. For this parameter, select only the browser that is installed and running on your operating system.
Clicking the New button displays a form for filling out a new interlocutor. See Figure 5.
Figure 5. New Interlocutor Profile
The "New Interlocutor Profile" section contains the main operating parameters:
Nickname - the name you choose for your interlocutor.
Language - the language your interlocutor speaks.
Description - a description of your interlocutor.
Use microphone - set the microphone usage flag.
Image - set the icon for the interlocutor.
If you have not selected an image for the interlocutor, the default image will be used for him.
The "Use microphone" parameter can be left unset for cases when you are only listening (news, audio lessons, etc.).
The "Help" section describes how to work with the program in your language. See Figure 6.
Figure 6. Help Section.
Clicking the "Exit" button terminates the program. See Figure 7.
Figure 7. Exit button.
Closing the browser window or closing the browser also ends the program.
Refreshing the page also ends the program.
On the left side of the browser is a list of your interlocutors. See Figure 8.
Figure 8. List of interlocutors.

For each interlocutor, their icon, date of the last message, and text of the last message in your language are shown. The date format throughout the task corresponds to your language. When you click on an interlocutor, their chat history is displayed.
The chat history of the interlocutor is located on the right side of the browser. See Figure 9.
Figure 9. Interlocutor's chat history.
The interlocutor's chat history is displayed when you click on the interlocutor in the left part.
The top menu contains:
Interlocutor's icon
Interlocutor's nickname
The "Start conversation" button
The "Clear history" button
The "Delete interlocutor" button
The "Interlocutor's profile" button
The chat history with the selected interlocutor appears in the right part.
The chat title is numbered with a pair of yaks: "Interlocutor's language"/"Your language".
The chat title contains the "Delete this chat" button.
When you click the "Delete this chat" button, this chat is deleted from the interlocutor's chat history.
All messages are divided into 2 groups:
Messages from the interlocutor.
Your messages.
Messages from the interlocutor are shifted to the left. Your messages are shifted to the right.
Each message group consists of 2 parts:
Message text in the language of the interlocutor, the color of this message is black.
Message text in your language, the color of this message is green.
Message date corresponds to your language.
Newer messages (by date) are located lower than older ones.

At the very bottom of the interlocutor's chats is the "Start conversation" button. When you click on it, the program starts calibrating the microphone and the sound device to determine the signal/noise levels and displays the corresponding message.
After calibrating the sound devices, the names of the buttons change: "Start conversation" -> "Stop conversation".
From this point on, you can start a conversation or launch any audio/video file to listen to.
All incoming messages (from the other person) will appear on the left.
All outgoing messages (from you) will appear on the right.
You will hear messages in your language and your other person will hear messages in their language.
At the end of the conversation/listening to the video, you need to click on one of the "Stop conversation" buttons.
The conversation also ends when:
Clicking on the other person in the left part of the browser in the list of interlocutors.
Clicking on the "New" button at the top of the list of interlocutors.
Clicking on the "Help" button in the top menu.
Clicking on the "Clear history" button in the top menu.
Clicking on the "Delete interlocutor" in the top menu.
By clicking on the "Interlocutor's profile" button in the top menu.
By clicking on the "Exit" button in the top menu.
The display of messages in the chat history can be adjusted by selecting the message display option in the upper left part of the menu (Green arrow).
When selecting "All languages" - the interlocutor's messages and your messages are displayed in all languages.
When selecting "My language" - the interlocutor's messages and your messages are displayed only in your language.
When selecting "Interlocutor's language" - the interlocutor's messages and your messages are displayed only in the interlocutor's language.
The default message display mode is "All languages".
When clicking on the "Clear history" button, all chats with the interlocutor are deleted, but the interlocutor remains. See Figure 10.
Figure 10. Clearing the history of the interlocutor.
If a chat with the interlocutor is currently in progress, it is stopped.
Clicking the "Delete interlocutor" button deletes all chats with the interlocutor and the interlocutor himself. See Figure 11.
Figure 11. Deleting the interlocutor.
If a chat with the interlocutor is currently in progress, it is stopped.
Clicking the "Interlocutor's profile" button displays a form filled with the interlocutor's data. See Figure 12.
Figure 12. Viewing the interlocutor's profile.
This item is similar to the item
The vertical separator allows you to change the width of the left and right areas of the workspace. See Figure 13.
Figure 13. The vertical separator of the left and right parts.
When you move the vertical separator, its position is saved. When you restart the program, the last position you set will be restored.
Move the mouse cursor over the speaker image in the lower right corner of the screen and right-click, then select the "Open Sound Settings" menu item, as shown in Figure 14.
Figure 14. Open Sound Settings.
A screen with the current sound settings will appear.
Figure 15. Current sound settings.
Restore the initial values ​​that were before starting the task. In your case, the names will be different.
Figure 16. Initial sound settings.
The system settings have been restored, you can work in Windows.
